task_completion.description: The agent sets the observer location to Jezero Crater, Mars with specific coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Location window is visible, with Planet set to 'Mars', Latitude to 18.44° N (or equivalent in degrees/minutes), Longitude to 77.45° E, and Elevation/Altitude to -2500m.

task_completion.description: The agent sets the simulation date and time to February 18, 2023, at 20:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Date and Time window is visible, showing the date as 2023-02-18 and time as 20:00:00.

task_completion.description: The agent configures the sky display for clear viewing by toggling specific settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium sky view shows a dark sky (atmosphere disabled), no ground/landscape blocking the view, and displays constellation lines, constellation names/labels, and planet labels.

task_completion.description: The agent observes Earth, Saturn, and a wide-field view of the Martian sky.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium view is seen centered on Earth, then Saturn, and also shows a zoomed-out wide-field view of the sky.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the required screenshots to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal shows the directory '/home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/' containing at least 3 image files, or the agent is seen using the screenshot tool to save them there.

task_completion.description: The agent creates the outreach planning file with the required information.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the file '/home/ga/Desktop/mars_sky_notes.txt'. The text includes the observer location (Mars/Jezero Crater), the date (Feb 18, 2023), and notes regarding the visibility of Earth and Saturn.

integrity.description: The agent interacts with the Stellarium graphical interface to set location, time, and display options.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking through Stellarium menus and windows rather than using terminal commands to modify configuration files.

integrity.description: The notes about Earth and Saturn visibility are based on actual observations in Stellarium.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent looks at Earth and Saturn in Stellarium before or while writing the notes, rather than writing generic statements without checking.

integrity.description: The agent uses a text editor to write the notes file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, or mousepad) is used to manually type or paste the notes, rather than a script generating the file automatically.
